title: Orbán Defeated in Hungary Election — NATO Blocker Removed Ahead of Helsingborg Summit
detail: Viktor Orbán conceded defeat in Hungary's parliamentary election after 16 years in power, with Péter Magyar's centre-right Tisza party securing 138 of 199 parliamentary seats on 53.6% of the vote. Orbán's Fidesz won just 55 seats with 37.8%. The result removes a named NATO blocker — Orbán had been the alliance's most obstructionist voice on Ukraine membership, US–Europe burden-sharing, and sanctions against Russia — and could fundamentally shift alliance consensus dynamics ahead of the Helsingborg NATO Foreign Ministers summit (May 21–22) and the Ankara summit (July 2026). Magyar has pledged to rebuild Hungary's frayed ties with NATO and the EU, potentially unlocking the €90 billion EU loan to Ukraine that Orbán had vetoed. Orbán's departure also severs Putin's main EU ally. NYT: 'four key takeaways' on what the vote means for the alliance and European security.

media.caption: Péter Magyar celebrates Tisza party victory in Budapest as Orbán concedes after 16 years in power
